title: Palak Khichdi

subs.name: fenugreek leaves (methi) for a slightly bitter twist

steps.text: Briefly blanch the spinach in boiling salted water for 1 minute, then drain and transfer to a blender with 2 tbsp water. Blend to a smooth puree and set aside. Blanching locks in the green colour.

Rinse rice and moong dal together until the water runs clear. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker over medium flame. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter, then add onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli and cook for 1 minute until the raw smell disappears. Add turmeric, mix, then add the rinsed rice and dal. Stir for 1 minute to coat with the ghee.
Add 3.5 cups of water and salt. Close the lid and cook on high until you get 2 whistles, then lower the heat and cook for 1 more whistle. Turn off and let the pressure drop naturally.

Open the cooker, stir gently. The khichdi should be soft and porridge-like. If too thick, add a splash of hot water. Stir in the spinach puree and garam masala over low heat and cook for 2 minutes until heated through.
Adjust salt, spoon into bowls and finish with a drizzle of ghee. Serve with curd, papad and a wedge of lemon. This is a wholesome, balanced meal as it is.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: spinach → fenugreek leaves (methi) for a slightly bitter twist; moong-dal → masoor dal (red lentil); ghee → oil
